I can’t diagnose the rash from a photo, and the safest answer is: don’t start an OTC cream until you know what it is. Some rashes get worse with the wrong product (e.g., steroid on a fungal infection, or antibiotic ointment on contact dermatitis).

What to do now
- Stop any new products, plants, or exposures on that area.
- Keep it clean and dry; wear loose, breathable clothing.
- Avoid scratching; trim nails.
- If it’s itchy, you can use a non-drowsy oral antihistamine (e.g., cetirizine or loratadine) and a cool compress.
- If it looks like simple dry/irritant dermatitis (red, dry, itchy, no blisters/oozing), a thin layer of 1% hydrocortisone cream twice daily for up to 7 days can help. Stop if it stings, spreads, or doesn’t improve in 3–5 days.
